rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek's point that hardcoding component names is fragile and the manager should instead derive from a single source of truth to handle naming/fusion conflicts) is closely echoed by X's comment 1 (derive from pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded list, cross-referencing offloader and registry.py), while Y's parallel finding (registry.py's transformer_attrs list has 'diverged' and deserves a clarifying comment) is a shallower, more cosmetic version of the same observation. X's two comments are also concretely actionable (specific file:line, specific code-level fix), whereas Y's output is mostly a validation checklist with one soft 'add a comment for future maintainers' suggestion. Both are well-grounded and accurate (they independently converge on the same registry.py:427 evidence), so precision is comparable, but X covers more of the real reviewer concern and is more actionable.
